advantages: Turns abstract high-dimensional structure into an intuitive picture—fast hypothesis generation for labels, batches, and errors.
Flexible metadata encodings (color, size, marker) support many analysis lenses on the same coordinate array.
Same plotting practices apply regardless of coordinate source (PCA, t-SNE, UMAP, neural embeddings).
Qualitative views complement numeric metrics—spatial context for confusion-matrix off-diagonal patterns.
disadvantages: Non-linear embeddings distort global distances—cluster size and inter-cluster gaps are not metrically faithful.
Overplotting and projection choices can hide or fabricate apparent structure on large datasets.
Visual clusters are subjective without quantitative clustering or stability checks.
Static 2D plots may miss structure visible only in higher dimensions or interactive rotation.
